schema.appDescription: Calculate total return on investment from a property — combining capital appreciation and rental income. See annualised ROI and compare with other asset classes.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Real Estate ROI Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is a good real estate ROI in India?
faq.a: Total ROI (capital appreciation + rental yield) for residential property in India: metro cities 9–12% p.a. over 10–20 years, Tier 2 cities 10–14%. But these are gross returns — deduct capital gains tax, maintenance, property tax, and broker fees for net return. Compare with Nifty 50 returning 13–15% CAGR historically.

faq.q: How is capital gains tax calculated on property sale?
faq.a: Holding ≤2 years: STCG (short-term capital gain) taxed at income slab rate. Holding >2 years: LTCG at 12.5% (no indexation) from Budget 2024, or optional 20% with indexation. Indexed cost = purchase price × (CII of sale year / CII of purchase year). Gain reinvested in new property or NHAI/REC bonds within 6 months qualifies for Section 54/54EC exemption.

faq.q: Should I include home loan interest in real estate ROI calculation?
faq.a: Yes. If the property is purchased with a home loan, the total cost = down payment + total EMIs paid + stamp duty/registration. Include all these in your cost basis for accurate ROI. The leveraged return can be higher or lower than the all-equity purchase return depending on loan rate vs appreciation.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹50 lakh property sold at ₹1 crore after 10 years
examples.text: Capital gain: ₹50 lakh. Rental income (₹15,000/month net for 10 years): ₹18 lakh. Total return: ₹68 lakh on ₹50 lakh investment. ROI: 136% total, CAGR ≈ 9.1% p.a. After LTCG tax (12.5% on ₹50 lakh = ₹6.25 lakh): net return ≈ 8.4% CAGR.

examples.heading: Leveraged purchase — 80% loan
examples.text: ₹50 lakh property, ₹10 lakh down payment (80% loan). Appreciation to ₹1 crore in 10 years: capital gain ₹50 lakh on ₹10 lakh invested = 500% gain (leveraged ROI). But subtract total interest paid (~₹40 lakh) = net ₹10 lakh gain on ₹10 lakh investment = moderate unleveraged return.
